assertions.text: Output contains a markdown table (using | column | syntax)
assertions.evidence: Full table starting with '| Scenario | Credit Score | Customer Age | Income Status | Credit Threshold (Policy) | Approved? | Reason? |'

assertions.text: At least one output column name ends with '?' (e.g. 'Eligible?' or 'Approved?')
Column names use domain/business language — no implementation terms like 'creditScore', 'isEligible', 'boolean', 'int'
assertions.evidence: Columns are: Scenario, Credit Score, Customer Age, Income Status, Credit Threshold (Policy), Approved?, Reason? — all domain language

assertions.text: Table includes at least one row specifically for the senior applicant (65+) threshold scenario
assertions.evidence: Row 'Senior applicant, above senior threshold' with Customer Age 65 and Credit Score 630 against threshold 600, and 'Senior applicant, at senior threshold' with Credit Score 600, Age 65

assertions.text: The missing income scenario is represented — either as a row with an open/uncertain expected value (?, TBD, or blank) or as an explicit open question note
assertions.evidence: Row 'Missing income information' has blank Income Status cell and 'Approved?' marked as '?' with note 'Open question — see below', plus dedicated Open Questions section discussing three resolution options

assertions.text: Scenario names describe conditions ('Senior applicant at threshold') not outcomes ('Approved')
assertions.evidence: Scenario names include 'Standard customer, above threshold', 'Senior applicant, at senior threshold', 'Missing income information', 'Senior applicant, just below senior age' — all describe the condition, not the outcome
